titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the G. & C. Merriam Company Records
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday February 5, 2019 at 04:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The records consist correspondence, advertisements, invoices, bills of lading, circulars and other papers relating to the G. & C. Merriam Company's wholesale and retail book trade. Includes records relating to Claremont Manufacturing Company and H.H. Bancroft and Company.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: This finding aid describes a collection that has been acquired by the library in discrete segments over time. The narrative sections of the finding aid provide information about the content and context of the collection. The Collection Contents section provides a general description and in some instances a box or folder listing of the various acquisitions of the collection. The Processing Notes section provides information about the way and extent to which the library has processed this collection. The Arrangement section provides information about how material within this collection is organized and tips on how to locate material across various acquisitions.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Eclectibles, 2010, on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, from M & S Rare Books, Inc., 2010, on the Hall Park McCullough Fund, and from Lawbook Exchange, Ltd., 2011, on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The G. & C. Merriam Company Records is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: G. & C. Merriam Company Records. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: G. & C. Merriam Company Archive. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: G. & C. Merriam Company
bioghist.p: The G. & C. Merriam Company, established by George and Charles Merriam in Springfield, Massachusetts, was the publisher and reviser of Noah Webster's American Dictionary beginning in 1847.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist correspondence, advertisements, invoices, bills of lading, circulars and other papers relating to the G. & C. Merriam Company's wholesale and retail book trade. Includes records relating to Claremont Manufacturing Company and H.H. Bancroft and Company.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: July and October 2010 Acquisitions, and August 2011 Acquisition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged in rough alphabetical order.

did.unittitle: A Key to Uncle Tom's Cabin - publicity flyer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a handwritten letter on the reverse.

did.unittitle: American Seamen's Friend Society - printed brochure with letter from L. P. Hubbard
A Book for the Million! - broadside circular
Books Published by T. R. Marvin - printed circular

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a handwritten letter on reverse from T.R. Marvin concerning a book order.

did.unittitle: Business cards, advertisements, promotional pamphlets, etc.
Business papers - H. H. Bancroft & Co.
Cole, Robert H. - letter requesting books
The Elements of Intellectual Philosophy - printed circular

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a handwritten letter on the reverse from Philips and Sampson.

did.unittitle: Jones, Henry and J. Hammond Trumbull - correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three G. & C. Merriam Co. key wallets.

unittitle.emph: List of Books Published and Offered to the Trade of John P. Jewett

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a bill for books ordered by Merriam, found on reverse.

did.unittitle: The Paramount Question! Is the Existence of a Privileged Class Compatible with the Theoretical Equality of Republican Institutions? - printed circular

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains five letters and two receipts.

did.unittitle: Program and menu for dinner to announce second edition
Prospectus of Lowe's Farm and Fireside Journal - printed circular

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from J. Payne Lowe on the reverse.

did.unittitle: Prospectus of the New York Recorder - printed ciruclar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a handwritten letter from James S. Dickinson & Co. on the reverse.

did.unittitle: Purchases - Hartford and New Haven - receipts
Sessions, William H. - letter concerning a debt
Shipping - Bills of Landing from Clipper Ships to San Francisco
Shipping - Bills of Landing - Railroads, Ships
Shipping - Railroads - Boston and Worcester Railroad - receipts
Shipping - Railroads - Conn. River Railroad Co. - receipts
Shipping - Steamboat - New Haven Steamboat Co. - receipts
Shipping - Transportation Lines - Union Transportation Line
Subscription to The Emancipator, New York - printed receipt
Warren, J.Q.A. - letters to Merriam and Advertising card
Works of Daniel Webster promotional proposal - circular

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a handwritten letter on the reverse, concerning a book order from Little Brown and Company.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, financial papers and printed material
